overview: Build the full React UI on top of the completed foundation. Covers the branded app shell, search and browse page with filters, asset detail modal, and the upload component with AI enrichment flow. Run this PRD in a feature/frontend worktree in parallel with the API PRD after the Foundation PRD has been merged.
goals: Royal Caribbean branded app shell with header and layout
Search and browse page with natural language search, filter sidebar, and asset grid
Asset detail modal showing original vs enriched metadata side by side with CDN copy
Upload component with drag-and-drop, folder support, and per-file enrichment status
nonGoals: No Express route implementation — that is the API PRD
No direct database access from the frontend
successMetrics: All 49 assets render in the grid on first load
Typing a search query filters the grid within 300ms debounce
Asset detail modal shows both original and enriched columns
A file can be drag-dropped, uploaded, and show Done status
All quality gates pass: npm run lint, npm run build, npm test
assumptions: Foundation PRD is complete and merged — scaffold, SQLite, and 49 ingested assets all exist
API PRD is complete or running in parallel — all /api/* endpoints are available at http://localhost:3001
client/ directory with Vite, React 18, axios, vitest, and @testing-library/react already installed

routes.name: Search & Browse
routes.purpose: Main page — search bar, filter sidebar, asset grid

uiNotes: Brand colors: Navy #001B6B (primary), Gold #C8960C (accent), LGRAY #F2F4F8 (card/sidebar bg), DGRAY #1A1A2E (text)
Header: fixed 64px, navy bg, gold 3px bottom border. Left: 'Royal Caribbean' bold white 20px + 'DAM Asset Intelligence' gold 12px below. Right: gold 'Upload' button
Layout: header fixed top, below = flex row. Left sidebar 260px fixed, right = fluid main with asset grid
Sidebar: white bg, 1px right border #E0E0E0, overflow-y auto. Sections: Category, Subcategory, Rights Status, Location, Metadata Quality
Asset card: white bg, 8px border-radius, box-shadow on hover, overflow hidden. Top: 16:9 img (object-fit cover, grey bg placeholder). Body: 10px padding, 2-line clamped title (13px bold), badges row
Rights dot: 8px circle — green #27AE60 (owned), amber #E67E22 (unclear), red #C0392B (none)
Warning icon: shown on card if quality_issues.length > 0 — amber triangle ⚠
Detail modal: fixed overlay full-screen, z-index 1000, semi-transparent black bg. White panel centered 90vw max-width 1100px, max-height 90vh, overflow auto
Modal left panel (40%): image, filename, dimensions, file size, format
Modal right panel (60%): two-column metadata table headers 'Original' | 'Enriched'. Enriched values in navy; grey dash if null. Gold left border on enriched cell if value differs from original
Quality Issues badges in modal: red bg for 'release_placeholder' and 'missing_rights'; amber for others
CDN URL: monospace code block, copy button — shows 'Copied!' for 2s
Upload modal: full-screen overlay. Drag-drop zone centered, dashed 2px border #C8960C, transitions to solid on dragover. File list below zone with per-file rows
File status colors: pending=grey, uploading=blue, enriching=purple, done=green #27AE60, error=red
Skeleton cards: grey animated shimmer rectangles matching card dimensions — show 12 while loading
rules: Thumbnail URL: if thumbnail_path set, request from GET http://localhost:3001/thumbnails/<thumbnail_path>; else show grey placeholder div
All API calls use baseURL http://localhost:3001
Search is debounced 300ms — do not fire a new API request on every keystroke
All active filters are combined into a single getAssets() call — one request per filter change
Parse display_tags from JSON string if it arrives as a string, else treat as array
Modal scroll lock: add overflow:hidden to body when modal is open, remove on close
ESC key closes any open modal (both detail and upload)
File size display: < 1MB show KB (e.g. '340 KB'), >= 1MB show MB (e.g. '4.6 MB')

stories.title: App shell, layout, and API client
stories.description: As a developer, I want a branded app shell with a consistent layout and a centralised API client so all pages share structure and API calls are in one place.
acceptanceCriteria: client/src/components/Header.jsx: fixed header 64px tall, navy bg (#001B6B), 3px gold bottom border. Left side: 'Royal Caribbean' white bold 20px, below it 'DAM Asset Intelligence' in gold 12px. Right side: Upload button in gold (#C8960C), white text, 10px 20px padding, border-radius 6px
client/src/App.jsx renders <Header onUploadClick={...} /> above a <main> content area with padding-top 64px to clear fixed header
client/src/api/assets.js exports: getAssets(params), getAsset(id), getFilters(), uploadFiles(formData), enrichAsset(id)
getAssets(params): GET /api/assets with params serialised as URLSearchParams, returns { total, assets }
getAsset(id): GET /api/assets/:id, returns full asset object
getFilters(): GET /api/filters, returns { categories, subcategories, locations, rights_statuses, counts }
uploadFiles(formData): POST /api/assets/upload with formData, returns { uploaded, assets }
enrichAsset(id): POST /api/assets/:id/enrich, returns updated asset object
All functions use axios instance with baseURL: 'http://localhost:3001'
Vitest test: Header renders text 'Royal Caribbean'
Vitest test: Header renders text 'DAM Asset Intelligence'
Vitest test: Header renders a button with text 'Upload'
Vitest test: getAssets called with { category: 'ships' } makes GET to /api/assets?category=ships (mock axios)
Negative case: axios network error propagates — callers handle it, app does not crash

stories.title: Search and browse page
stories.description: As a content author, I want to search and browse assets on the main page with live filters so I can find the right image quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: client/src/pages/BrowsePage.jsx is the main page rendered at route /

client/src/components/SearchBar.jsx: text input full-width, placeholder 'Search by keyword, location, mood, ship...', onChange updates q state, debounced 300ms using setTimeout/clearTimeout in useEffect

client/src/components/FilterSidebar.jsx: 260px fixed left panel. Sections (each collapsible with a toggle arrow): Category (ships/promotions chips from getFilters()), Subcategory (dynamic chips under selected category), Rights Status (owned/unclear/none chips), Location (select dropdown from getFilters()), Metadata Quality (toggle chips: 'Missing Title', 'Missing Rights', 'Release Placeholder')
Active filters shown as removable chips in a row below SearchBar. Each chip has an × button that clears that filter
All filter state managed in BrowsePage, passed to getAssets() as params on any change
client/src/components/AssetGrid.jsx: CSS grid, repeat(auto-fill, minmax(200px, 1fr)), gap 16px

client/src/components/AssetCard.jsx: white card, 8px border-radius, hover shadow. Top: aspect-ratio 16/9 img with object-fit cover (grey #E8E8E8 bg if no thumbnail). Body: 10px padding, 2-line clamped display_title (13px bold #1A1A2E), badges row showing category pill and rights dot, amber ⚠ icon if quality_issues.length > 0
Results count label above grid: 'Showing {assets.length} of {total} assets' in grey 12px
Loading state: show 12 SkeletonCard components (grey animated shimmer matching card shape) while API call is in-flight
Empty state: centred message 'No assets match your search.' with a 'Clear all filters' button that resets all filter state
Clicking an AssetCard calls onSelectAsset(asset.id) which is wired to open AssetDetailModal
Vitest test: BrowsePage renders 3 AssetCards when getAssets mock returns 3 assets
Vitest test: typing 'sunset' in SearchBar triggers getAssets with q='sunset' after debounce (use fake timers)
Vitest test: clicking category chip 'ships' calls getAssets with category='ships'
Vitest test: clicking active filter chip × removes it and calls getAssets without that param
Vitest test: shows 12 skeleton cards while loading is true
Negative case: getAssets rejects → error banner 'Failed to load assets. Please try again.' shown above grid, no crash

stories.title: Asset detail modal
stories.description: As a content author, I want a full-detail popup for any asset so I can compare original vs enriched metadata, copy the CDN URL, and trigger AI enrichment.
acceptanceCriteria: client/src/components/AssetDetailModal.jsx renders as a fixed full-screen overlay when selectedAssetId is non-null
Modal fetches full asset via getAsset(id) on open — shows loading spinner until resolved
Overlay: fixed, inset 0, z-index 1000, background rgba(0,0,0,0.6). Inner panel: white, border-radius 12px, max-width 1100px, 90vw, max-height 90vh, overflow-y auto, centred with flex
Close button × at top-right — closes modal. ESC keydown listener added on mount, removed on unmount
body overflow:hidden added when modal opens, restored on close

Left panel (40% width): image tag src=web_image_path or thumbnail_path (proxied through Express /thumbnails/), grey placeholder if both null. Below image: filename in bold, 'W × H px' dimensions, formatted file size, format badge

Right panel (60% width): scrollable. Metadata table — two columns with headers 'Original' and 'Enriched' in navy. Rows: Title, Description, Tags, Location, Creator, Rights Owner, Usage Terms, Channel, Format. Cell values: enriched column shows enriched_* value in navy; null shows grey '—'. If enriched value is non-null AND differs from original: add 3px left border gold on enriched cell

Quality Issues section below table: only renders if quality_issues.length > 0. Each issue shown as a badge: 'release_placeholder' and 'missing_rights' → red badge; others → amber badge. Badge labels: missing_title='Missing Title', missing_rights='No Rights Data', release_placeholder='Release Placeholder — Review Required', s7_sync_error='CDN Sync Error', etc.

CDN URL section: renders only if cdn_url is non-null. Shows label 'CDN URL', full url in <code> monospace block, Copy button. On Copy click: navigator.clipboard.writeText(cdn_url), button text → 'Copied!' for 2000ms then reverts to 'Copy'

'Enrich with AI' button shown only if enrichment_source is null or 'pending'. On click: calls enrichAsset(id), button shows spinner + 'Enriching...', on success re-fetches asset and refreshes modal, on 503 shows toast 'Enrichment unavailable — add OPENAI_API_KEY to .env'
Vitest test: modal renders display_title from mocked getAsset response
Vitest test: ESC keydown triggers onClose
Vitest test: clicking × button triggers onClose
Vitest test: Copy button shows 'Copied!' immediately after click (mock clipboard API)
Vitest test: 'Enrich with AI' button is visible when enrichment_source is null
Vitest test: quality_issues badge 'No Rights Data' renders when 'missing_rights' in quality_issues
Negative case: cdn_url is null → CDN URL section is not rendered
Negative case: enrichAsset returns 503 → toast shown, modal remains open

stories.title: Upload component with per-file enrichment status
stories.description: As a content author, I want to drag-and-drop or browse for images and have them automatically enriched with AI metadata so new assets are immediately usable in the library.
acceptanceCriteria: client/src/components/UploadModal.jsx renders as a fixed full-screen overlay when isOpen is true
Overlay same style as AssetDetailModal. Header: 'Upload Assets' title, × close button. ESC closes modal

Drag-and-drop zone: centred box, dashed 2px border #C8960C, border-radius 8px, 200px min-height, text 'Drop images here or click to browse'. On dragover: border becomes solid, bg tints gold lightly. On drop: captures files
Hidden <input type='file' accept='image/jpeg,image/png,image/webp' multiple ref> triggered when zone is clicked
Toggle button below zone: 'Upload folder' — switches input to include webkitdirectory and multiple attributes. Toggle label changes to 'Upload files' when folder mode is active
After file selection, file list renders below zone. Each row: filename (truncated 40 chars), formatted file size, status badge

Client-side validation before adding to list: file > 20MB → status 'Too large (max 20MB)' red, excluded from upload. Non-image MIME → status 'Unsupported type' red, excluded. Valid files → status 'Pending' grey
Submit button 'Upload {N} files' at bottom of modal — disabled if no valid (pending) files
On submit: build FormData appending all valid files under field 'files', call uploadFiles(formData). Each file status → 'Uploading...' blue
On upload success: for each { id } in response.assets, immediately call enrichAsset(id). File status → 'Enriching...' purple
On enrich success: file status → 'Done ✓' green
On enrich 503: file status → amber 'Uploaded — enrichment unavailable'
When ALL files reach a terminal status (done/error/unavailable): 'View in library' button appears. On click: close modal, call onUploadComplete() which triggers getAssets() refresh in BrowsePage
Vitest test: dropping a valid JPEG sets its status to 'Pending' in the file list
Vitest test: file > 20MB shows 'Too large' status and is not in the pending list
Vitest test: Submit button is disabled when file list is empty
Vitest test: Submit button label shows correct file count 'Upload 3 files'
Vitest test: after mocked uploadFiles + enrichAsset resolve, file status shows 'Done'
Vitest test: 'View in library' button appears after all files are done
Negative case: uploadFiles rejects for one file → that file shows 'Upload failed' red, other files continue processing independently
Negative case: enrichAsset returns 503 → file shows amber 'Uploaded — enrichment unavailable', 'View in library' still appears when all files settle
